text.title: jira-workflow-management Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for jira-workflow-management. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Limit workflows to 5-7 statuses maximum; use one shared workflow per issue type across projects rather than bespoke per-project workflows.
rule.rationale: Per-project workflow proliferation creates scheme sprawl, breaks cross-project JQL queries, and slows the entire Jira instance through scheme-lookup overhead.

rule.statement: Place required field validators on transitions, not on issue creation; this reduces friction for bulk imports and agent-driven flows without sacrificing data quality at the stage where it matters.
rule.rationale: Creation-time required fields block bulk migrations and bot-driven flows unless service accounts are explicitly exempted.

rule.statement: Resist creating new issue types for every workflow nuance; add a custom field or label instead.
rule.rationale: New issue types require scheme updates, affect all projects using the shared scheme, and multiply configuration complexity without proportional value.

rule.statement: Limit custom fields aggressively; share them via Global context rather than project-scoped context; Jira has a 200-field issue-view cap and performance degrades well before it.
rule.rationale: Custom field sprawl is the most common cause of Jira performance degradation in large instances. Each field adds a database column that is loaded even when not displayed.

rule.statement: Test automation rules in a sandbox project with a copy of production schemes before promoting; tag every automation-created issue with an "automation" label for audit.
rule.rationale: Automation rules that fire in production before testing can transition thousands of issues incorrectly in seconds. Sandbox validation catches logic errors safely.

rule.statement: Add cycle guards to all automation rules; a rule that triggers rule B that triggers rule A creates infinite loops that burn the monthly automation budget instantly.
rule.rationale: Jira Cloud enforces per-tier monthly automation execution limits. Loops exhaust the budget within minutes, silencing all automations for the rest of the month.

rule.statement: Validate JQL queries with "validateQuery=strict" in the REST API before storing or executing them; never assume a JQL string from an LLM is valid.
rule.rationale: LLMs frequently hallucinate Jira field names. The strict validator returns field-level errors that allow targeted correction before silent failures in production.

rule.statement: Use "smart commits" to link branches via "PROJ-123" in commit messages; do not rely on humans to manually link PRs to issues after the fact.
rule.rationale: Manual PR-to-issue linking has near-zero compliance on active teams. Smart commits wire the link at source without workflow friction.

rule.statement: Convert agent-generated Markdown to ADF (Atlassian Document Format) before writing to Jira description or comment fields; Markdown written directly produces empty or malformed fields.
rule.rationale: Jira's description field accepts HTML/ADF, not Markdown. Agents writing raw Markdown silently produce fields with visible Markdown syntax or empty content.

rule.statement: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints are mandatory at: workflow promotion to production, automation rule promotion, schema changes, and bulk transitions affecting more than 100 issues.
rule.rationale: These operations affect all team members simultaneously and are expensive or impossible to reverse.
